task_completion.description: The FZK-Haus IFC model is loaded and visible in the Blender viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender 3D viewport displays a building model (FZK-Haus).

task_completion.description: A wall element is selected in the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A wall element is highlighted (usually with an orange outline) in the 3D viewport, or explicitly selected in the Blender Outliner panel.

task_completion.description: The IFC Class and Name of the selected wall are visible in the properties panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties tab is open in Blender, displaying the BlenderBIM IFC Object metadata. The 'Class' field shows 'IfcWall' or 'IfcWallStandardCase', and the 'Name' field is populated.

task_completion.description: The GlobalId of the selected wall is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlenderBIM IFC Object properties panel clearly displays the 'GlobalId' string for the selected wall.

task_completion.description: The property sets, specifically Pset_WallCommon, are visible for the selected wall.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Property Sets section in the BlenderBIM panel is expanded, showing 'Pset_WallCommon' and its associated properties for the selected wall.

integrity.description: The agent used the BlenderBIM GUI to inspect properties.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the Blender interface with the BlenderBIM add-on panels being used, rather than a terminal or text editor reading the raw .ifc file.

integrity.description: The properties displayed belong to a genuine wall element from the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: The properties panel corresponds to the actively selected object in the 3D viewport or Outliner, not a manually created dummy object or text note.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the BIM software by opening the IFC file as plain text.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like Notepad, VS Code, or Vim) open displaying the raw STEP/IFC code to find the GlobalId or Pset.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The model is 'AC20-FZK-Haus.ifc' (FZK-Haus reference building). It contains exactly 13 IfcWallStandardCase elements. The VLM should verify that the selected element's class is IfcWallStandardCase (or IfcWall) and that the standard IFC properties (Name, GlobalId, Pset_WallCommon) are actively displayed in the BlenderBIM Object Properties interface.
